member.name: T:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1
member.summary: A bounded, lock-free multi-producer / single-consumer (MPSC) ring buffer based on Dmitry Vyukov's sequenced-cell bounded queue, restricted to a single consumer so the dequeue path needs no CAS at all.
member.typeparam: The element type. Slots holding reference types are cleared on dequeue.

remarks.para: Threading contract: any number of threads may call concurrently. Exactly one thread may call and . The enqueue path is lock-free (a failed CAS means another producer made progress); the dequeue path is wait-free.
Allocation behavior: the only managed allocation is the cell array created in the constructor. Enqueue/dequeue are allocation-free.

Each cell carries a sequence number used as a turn indicator: a cell whose sequence equals the producer's claimed position is free to write; a cell whose sequence equals position + 1 is full and ready to read. After consumption the consumer advances the cell's sequence by the full capacity so the cell becomes writable for the next lap.

member.name: T:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Cell
member.summary: A storage cell pairing the payload with its lap-sequence number.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Cell.Sequence
member.summary: Turn indicator; see the class remarks for the protocol.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Cell.Item
member.summary: The stored payload (valid only when the sequence marks the cell as full).

member.name: T:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Cursors
member.summary: Producer/consumer cursors padded onto separate cache lines.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Cursors.Tail
member.summary: Next position producers will claim (CAS-incremented).

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Cursors.Head
member.summary: Next position the consumer will read. Written only by the consumer.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new ring buffer with at least slots. The actual capacity is rounded up to the next power of two.
member.param: Minimum number of slots; must be between 2 and 2^30 inclusive.
member.exception: is less than 2 or greater than 2^30.

member.name: P:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the fixed capacity of the buffer (always a power of two).

member.name: P:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.Count
member.summary: Gets an approximate count of items currently in the buffer. Best-effort under concurrency.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.TryEnqueue(`0@)
member.summary: Attempts to enqueue an item. Safe to call from any number of producer threads.
member.returns: if the item was enqueued; if the buffer was full at the time of the attempt.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.TryDequeue(`0@)
member.summary: Attempts to dequeue an item. Consumer thread only. Wait-free.
member.param: Receives the dequeued item, or default if no item is ready.
member.returns: if an item was dequeued; otherwise .

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.MpscRingBuffer`1.DequeueBatch(System.Span{`0})
member.summary: Dequeues up to .Length ready items. Consumer thread only.
member.returns: The number of items dequeued (0 if no items were ready).

member.name: T:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1
member.summary: A bounded, lock-free, wait-free single-producer / single-consumer (SPSC) ring buffer.
member.typeparam: The element type. Both value types and reference types are supported; slots holding reference types are cleared on dequeue so the buffer never extends object lifetimes.

remarks.para: Threading contract: exactly one thread may call the producer methods () and exactly one thread may call the consumer methods (, , ). Producer and consumer may be (and usually are) different threads. Violating this contract produces undefined results.

Allocation behavior: the only managed allocation is the backing array created in the constructor. All enqueue/dequeue operations are allocation-free and never take locks; they use a single volatile store for publication, plus cached views of the opposite index so that the common case touches only cache lines owned by the calling thread.
Indices are monotonically increasing 64-bit sequence numbers; at one billion operations per second a wraparound would take roughly 292 years, so overflow is not handled.

member.name: T:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Indices
member.summary: Producer- and consumer-owned indices, padded onto distinct cache lines to eliminate false sharing between the two threads.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Indices.Tail
member.summary: Next sequence number the producer will write. Written only by the producer.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Indices.CachedHead
member.summary: Producer's cached view of . Written only by the producer.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Indices.Head
member.summary: Next sequence number the consumer will read. Written only by the consumer.

member.name: F:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Indices.CachedTail
member.summary: Consumer's cached view of . Written only by the consumer.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new ring buffer with at least slots. The actual capacity is rounded up to the next power of two.
member.param: Minimum number of slots; must be between 1 and 2^30 inclusive.
member.exception: is less than 1 or greater than 2^30.

member.name: P:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Capacity
member.summary: Gets the fixed capacity of the buffer (always a power of two).

member.name: P:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.Count
member.summary: Gets an approximate count of items currently in the buffer. The value is exact only when observed from a quiescent state; under concurrent use it is a best-effort snapshot. This property is allocation-free.

member.name: P:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.IsEmpty
member.summary: Gets a best-effort indication of whether the buffer is empty.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.TryEnqueue(`0@)
member.summary: Attempts to enqueue an item. Producer thread only.
member.returns: if the item was enqueued; if the buffer is full.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.TryDequeue(`0@)
member.summary: Attempts to dequeue an item. Consumer thread only.
member.param: Receives the dequeued item, or default if the buffer is empty.
member.returns: if an item was dequeued; if the buffer is empty.

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.TryPeek(`0@)
member.summary: Attempts to read the next item without removing it. Consumer thread only.
member.param: Receives the next item, or default if the buffer is empty.
member.returns: if an item is available; otherwise .

member.name: M:ZeroAlloc.Concurrency.SpscRingBuffer`1.DequeueBatch(System.Span{`0})
member.summary: Dequeues up to .Length items into the destination span in a single pass, amortizing the publication cost. Consumer thread only.
member.returns: The number of items actually dequeued (0 if the buffer is empty).
